title: Belgium Schengen Tourist / Short-Stay Visa
snippet: Belgium is a full member of the Schengen Area. A single Type C visa lets you stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period across all Schengen countries, for tourism, family visits or business.
keywords: Belgium is a full member of the Schengen Area. A single Type C visa lets you stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period across all Schengen countries, for tourism, family visits or business.
Typically 15 calendar days from a complete application; can extend up to 30-60 days during peak season or if additional checks are required.
Up to 90 days within any 180-day period, multiple-entry depending on issuance.

title: Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Belgium?
snippet: Yes. Indian passport holders need a Schengen Type C visa for tourism or short business visits of up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Apply through VFS Global.
keywords: Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Belgium?
Yes. Indian passport holders need a Schengen Type C visa for tourism or short business visits of up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Apply through VFS Global.

title: How long does a Belgium visa take to process?
snippet: Typically around 15 calendar days for a complete application, but it can take up to 30-60 days during busy periods, so apply as early as possible (up to 6 months ahead, and no later than 15 days before travel).
keywords: How long does a Belgium visa take to process?

Typically around 15 calendar days for a complete application, but it can take up to 30-60 days during busy periods, so apply as early as possible (up to 6 months ahead, and no later than 15 days before travel).

title: What is the emergency number in Belgium?
snippet: Dial 112 for any emergency (ambulance, fire or police) anywhere in Belgium — it's toll-free. You can also dial 101 directly for police or 100 for ambulance/fire.
keywords: What is the emergency number in Belgium?
Dial 112 for any emergency (ambulance, fire or police) anywhere in Belgium — it's toll-free. You can also dial 101 directly for police or 100 for ambulance/fire.

title: Where is the Indian Embassy in Belgium located?
snippet: The Embassy of India is at 217 Chaussée de Vleurgat, 1050 Brussels. It covers Belgium, Luxembourg and the EU institutions.
keywords: Where is the Indian Embassy in Belgium located?
The Embassy of India is at 217 Chaussée de Vleurgat, 1050 Brussels. It covers Belgium, Luxembourg and the EU institutions.

title: How do I get from Brussels Airport into the city?
snippet: The Brussels Airport Express train takes about 18 minutes to Brussels-Central (~€8.90). The STIB Airport Bus (Line 12) is a cheaper alternative into the EU Quarter (~€7.90 with a MOBIB or contactless card).
keywords: How do I get from Brussels Airport into the city?

The Brussels Airport Express train takes about 18 minutes to Brussels-Central (~€8.90). The STIB Airport Bus (Line 12) is a cheaper alternative into the EU Quarter (~€7.90 with a MOBIB or contactless card).

title: What currency is used in Belgium?
snippet: The Euro (EUR). Contactless card payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are common in all cities.
keywords: What currency is used in Belgium?
The Euro (EUR). Contactless card payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are common in all cities.

title: Is Belgium safe for solo travellers?
snippet: Belgium is generally very safe. As in any European city, be alert for pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas and on public transport.
keywords: Is Belgium safe for solo travellers?
Belgium is generally very safe. As in any European city, be alert for pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas and on public transport.

title: Can I find vegetarian and Jain food in Belgium?
snippet: Yes, especially in Brussels and Antwerp (particularly around the Diamond District), which have dedicated Indian vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants.
keywords: Can I find vegetarian and Jain food in Belgium?
Yes, especially in Brussels and Antwerp (particularly around the Diamond District), which have dedicated Indian vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants.

title: What languages are spoken in Belgium?
snippet: Belgium has three official languages: Dutch (Flanders), French (Wallonia) and German (small eastern region). Brussels is officially bilingual French/Dutch. English is widely understood in cities.
keywords: What languages are spoken in Belgium?
Belgium has three official languages: Dutch (Flanders), French (Wallonia) and German (small eastern region). Brussels is officially bilingual French/Dutch. English is widely understood in cities.

title: Do I need to register with the local municipality after arriving?
snippet: Yes, if staying longer than 90 days, non-EU nationals must register their address at the local commune/gemeente (municipality) within 8 working days of arrival.
keywords: Do I need to register with the local municipality after arriving?
Yes, if staying longer than 90 days, non-EU nationals must register their address at the local commune/gemeente (municipality) within 8 working days of arrival.

title: How do public transport tickets work across Belgium?
snippet: Each region has its own operator — STIB (Brussels), De Lijn (Flanders), TEC (Wallonia) and SNCB (national trains). MOBIB cards and contactless bank cards work on most local networks; SNCB tickets are separate.
keywords: How do public transport tickets work across Belgium?

Each region has its own operator — STIB (Brussels), De Lijn (Flanders), TEC (Wallonia) and SNCB (national trains). MOBIB cards and contactless bank cards work on most local networks; SNCB tickets are separate.

title: What should I pack for Belgium's weather?
snippet: Belgium has a mild, rainy maritime climate. Pack layers, a waterproof jacket, and comfortable walking shoes year-round; winters are cold and grey, summers are mild.
keywords: What should I pack for Belgium's weather?
Belgium has a mild, rainy maritime climate. Pack layers, a waterproof jacket, and comfortable walking shoes year-round; winters are cold and grey, summers are mild.

title: Can I use my Indian driving licence in Belgium?
snippet: An Indian driving licence with an International Driving Permit (IDP) can be used for short stays (up to 185 days). For residents, conversion to a Belgian licence is required within that window.
keywords: Can I use my Indian driving licence in Belgium?
An Indian driving licence with an International Driving Permit (IDP) can be used for short stays (up to 185 days). For residents, conversion to a Belgian licence is required within that window.

title: Are there Hindu temples or gurudwaras in Belgium?
snippet: Yes — Brussels and Antwerp have Hindu temples and gurudwaras serving the Indian community. See the Indian Community section for details.
keywords: Are there Hindu temples or gurudwaras in Belgium?
Yes — Brussels and Antwerp have Hindu temples and gurudwaras serving the Indian community. See the Indian Community section for details.

title: How do I renew my Indian passport while in Belgium?
snippet: Passport renewal is handled by the Embassy of India in Brussels. Check indianembassybrussels.gov.in for the latest application forms and appointment system.
keywords: How do I renew my Indian passport while in Belgium?
Passport renewal is handled by the Embassy of India in Brussels. Check indianembassybrussels.gov.in for the latest application forms and appointment system.

title: Is healthcare good in Belgium?
snippet: Belgium has a high-quality healthcare system. Visitors should carry travel insurance; residents must enrol in mandatory health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds).
keywords: Belgium has a high-quality healthcare system. Visitors should carry travel insurance; residents must enrol in mandatory health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds).

title: Can I work in Belgium on a tourist visa?
snippet: No. A Schengen tourist visa does not permit employment. You need a work permit or single permit tied to a specific job offer.
keywords: Can I work in Belgium on a tourist visa?
No. A Schengen tourist visa does not permit employment. You need a work permit or single permit tied to a specific job offer.

title: What is the best way to travel between Belgian cities?
snippet: SNCB trains are fast, frequent and connect all major cities — usually the best option over driving, especially between Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent and Bruges.
keywords: What is the best way to travel between Belgian cities?
SNCB trains are fast, frequent and connect all major cities — usually the best option over driving, especially between Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent and Bruges.

title: Do I need an adapter for electrical outlets in Belgium?
snippet: Belgium uses the Type E plug (230V, 50Hz), the same as most of continental Europe. Indian plugs (Type C/D) will need an adapter.
keywords: Do I need an adapter for electrical outlets in Belgium?
Belgium uses the Type E plug (230V, 50Hz), the same as most of continental Europe. Indian plugs (Type C/D) will need an adapter.

title: How do I find English-speaking doctors in Belgium?
snippet: Many doctors in Brussels and other major cities speak English, especially in international clinics. See the Healthcare section for guidance on finding one.
keywords: How do I find English-speaking doctors in Belgium?
Many doctors in Brussels and other major cities speak English, especially in international clinics. See the Healthcare section for guidance on finding one.

title: Belgium Schengen visa fee guidance updated
snippet: VFS Global centres in India continue to process Belgium short-stay visa applications; standard processing remains around 15 days for complete applications.
keywords: Belgium Schengen visa fee guidance updated
VFS Global centres in India continue to process Belgium short-stay visa applications; standard processing remains around 15 days for complete applications.

title: Brussels Airport Express train remains fastest airport link
snippet: The Airport Express train continues to be the quickest way from Brussels Airport into the city centre, taking around 18 minutes to Brussels-Central.
keywords: Brussels Airport Express train remains fastest airport link
The Airport Express train continues to be the quickest way from Brussels Airport into the city centre, taking around 18 minutes to Brussels-Central.

snippet: Indian associations in Brussels and Antwerp are preparing community Diwali events — check the Community & Festivals section closer to the date.

title: STIB MOBIB card contactless payment expanding
snippet: Contactless bank card payment continues to roll out across STIB buses and trams as an alternative to the MOBIB card.
keywords: STIB MOBIB card contactless payment expanding
Contactless bank card payment continues to roll out across STIB buses and trams as an alternative to the MOBIB card.

title: New academic year: student visa reminders for KU Leuven and other universities
snippet: Indian students admitted to Belgian universities are reminded to apply for their student visa well ahead of the academic year start.
keywords: New academic year: student visa reminders for KU Leuven and other universities
Indian students admitted to Belgian universities are reminded to apply for their student visa well ahead of the academic year start.

title: Sample alert: check for seasonal SNCB engineering works
snippet: SNCB periodically runs weekend engineering works affecting specific lines. This is a sample entry — replace with a live advisory when relevant.

title: Sample alert: verify current customs allowances before travel
snippet: Duty-free allowances can change; always check the Belgian customs website close to your travel date. This is a sample placeholder entry.

title: Brussels Mandir (Indian Cultural Centre asbl)
snippet: A Hindu temple established by the Indian community in Brussels, soft-opened in February 2012. Run by the Indian Cultural Centre asbl.
keywords: A Hindu temple established by the Indian community in Brussels, soft-opened in February 2012. Run by the Indian Cultural Centre asbl.

title: Ram Mandir Brussels
snippet: The oldest Hindu temple in Brussels, established over 30 years ago in Etterbeek.
keywords: The oldest Hindu temple in Brussels, established over 30 years ago in Etterbeek.

title: Shankheshwar Parshvanath Jain Temple
snippet: A Jain temple serving Antwerp's Gujarati Jain community, many of whom trace their roots to Palanpur, Gujarat, and work in the diamond trade.
keywords: A Jain temple serving Antwerp's Gujarati Jain community, many of whom trace their roots to Palanpur, Gujarat, and work in the diamond trade.

title: BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir Antwerp
snippet: Part of the international BAPS Swaminarayan network, serving the Hindu community in Antwerp.
keywords: Part of the international BAPS Swaminarayan network, serving the Hindu community in Antwerp.

title: Gurdwara Sangat Sahib
snippet: The largest gurudwara in Belgium, built in 2021 in traditional Indian architectural style with capacity for around 1,000 worshippers. Sint-Truiden and the surrounding Haspengouw region are home to Belgium's largest Sikh community, historically drawn by seasonal fruit-picking work.
keywords: The largest gurudwara in Belgium, built in 2021 in traditional Indian architectural style with capacity for around 1,000 worshippers. Sint-Truiden and the surrounding Haspengouw region are home to Belgium's largest Sikh community, historically drawn by seasonal fruit-picking work.

title: Gurdwara Guru Nanak Sahib Cultureel Centrum
snippet: Established in 1999 in a converted cinema, serving the Sikh community in and around Brussels.
keywords: Gurdwara Guru Nanak Sahib Cultureel Centrum
Established in 1999 in a converted cinema, serving the Sikh community in and around Brussels.

title: Indian Association of Belgium
snippet: A community association aiming to celebrate Indian cultural heritage and foster connections between Indians and Belgians.
keywords: A community association aiming to celebrate Indian cultural heritage and foster connections between Indians and Belgians.

title: Antwerp Indian Association
snippet: Founded in 1979, representing the Indian community in Antwerp, historically closely tied to the diamond trade.
keywords: Founded in 1979, representing the Indian community in Antwerp, historically closely tied to the diamond trade.

title: ABC – A Bollywood Company
snippet: Belgium's only Indian-led Indian dance company, led by artistic director Swapnil Dagliya, offering Bollywood, Bhangra, Garba and Indian folk dance performances and classes. Organises the annual Gent India Dans Festival (GIDF).
keywords: Belgium's only Indian-led Indian dance company, led by artistic director Swapnil Dagliya, offering Bollywood, Bhangra, Garba and Indian folk dance performances and classes. Organises the annual Gent India Dans Festival (GIDF).

title: Bollylicious

keywords: An Indian dance collective founded by four Belgian dancers, presenting Bollywood and fusion Indian dance across Belgium.

title: International Protestant Church of Brussels (IPC)
snippet: An international, interdenominational congregation attended by many Indian Christian families in Brussels; no dedicated Indian-specific congregation was identified, so this and other international churches are the most common option for Indian Christians in the city.
keywords: An international, interdenominational congregation attended by many Indian Christian families in Brussels; no dedicated Indian-specific congregation was identified, so this and other international churches are the most common option for Indian Christians in the city.

keywords: Festival of lights, celebrated with community events at temples such as Brussels Mandir and Ram Mandir, and among Antwerp's Gujarati community.

keywords: Flag hoisting and cultural events, typically held at or in coordination with the Embassy of India in Brussels.

title: Navratri / Garba

keywords: Royal Museums of Fine Arts of Belgium

title: Grand Curtius Museum

keywords: Art, archaeology and decorative arts museum housed in a historic mansion overlooking the Meuse river.

title: Gravensteen Castle

keywords: A fairy-tale 16th-century château in the village of Gaasbeek, a short trip from Brussels.

keywords: One of the largest fortress complexes in Europe, with ~7km of underground tunnels and a cable car link to the city centre.

keywords: A grand park in the EU Quarter with a triumphal arch, museums and frequent summer festivals and concerts.

keywords: A large landscaped park designed in 1861 at the edge of the Sonian Forest, with a lake, boating, and the Le Chalet Robinson café on its island.

keywords: Renowned Brussels chocolatier on Place du Grand Sablon, sourcing cocoa beans globally and controlling the whole bean-to-bar process.

keywords: A Bruges chocolatier known for smooth, creamy pralines, located along Katelijnestraat's cluster of chocolate shops.

title: Delirium Café
snippet: Holds a Guinness World Record for beer selection (2,004 beers in 2004), including Belgian Trappist beers such as Rochefort 8/12 and Westmalle Tripel, in an 18th-century cellar near the Grand Place.
keywords: Holds a Guinness World Record for beer selection (2,004 beers in 2004), including Belgian Trappist beers such as Rochefort 8/12 and Westmalle Tripel, in an 18th-century cellar near the Grand Place.

title: Bruges day trip from Brussels
snippet: About 1 hour by direct train — canals, chocolate shops and the historic centre in a single day.
keywords: About 1 hour by direct train — canals, chocolate shops and the historic centre in a single day.

title: Leuven day trip from Brussels
snippet: Only ~25 minutes by train — the KU Leuven library, Oude Markt and botanical garden make for an easy half-day or full-day trip.
keywords: Only ~25 minutes by train — the KU Leuven library, Oude Markt and botanical garden make for an easy half-day or full-day trip.

title: Mechelen day trip from Brussels or Antwerp
snippet: 20-25 minutes from either city — St. Rumbold's Tower and two UNESCO béguinages in a compact, walkable centre.
keywords: Mechelen day trip from Brussels or Antwerp

title: Durbuy
snippet: A tiny medieval town in the Ardennes, popularly promoted as 'the smallest city in the world', with cobblestone streets and artisanal shops.
keywords: A tiny medieval town in the Ardennes, popularly promoted as 'the smallest city in the world', with cobblestone streets and artisanal shops.

title: Kortrijk
snippet: A lesser-visited city blending medieval architecture (Broel Towers) with a contemporary arts scene on Buda Island.
keywords: A lesser-visited city blending medieval architecture (Broel Towers) with a contemporary arts scene on Buda Island.

title: Health Insurance
snippet: Belgium has mandatory health insurance for residents through a 'mutualité'/'ziekenfonds'. Visitors should carry travel medical insurance covering at least €30,000.
keywords: Belgium has mandatory health insurance for residents through a 'mutualité'/'ziekenfonds'. Visitors should carry travel medical insurance covering at least €30,000.

title: Finding a GP (huisarts/médecin généraliste)
snippet: Registering with a local GP gives you a consistent point of contact and referrals to specialists.
keywords: Registering with a local GP gives you a consistent point of contact and referrals to specialists.

title: Hospitals
snippet: Belgium has a high standard of hospital care. Major university hospitals with international patient services include Cliniques universitaires Saint-Luc, H.U.B (Hôpital Universitaire de Bruxelles), CHIREC and Erasmus Hospital in Brussels, and Antwerp University Hospital (UZA) in Antwerp.
keywords: Belgium has a high standard of hospital care. Major university hospitals with international patient services include Cliniques universitaires Saint-Luc, H.U.B (Hôpital Universitaire de Bruxelles), CHIREC and Erasmus Hospital in Brussels, and Antwerp University Hospital (UZA) in Antwerp.

title: Pharmacies
snippet: Pharmacies (apotheek/pharmacie) are widely available and marked with a green cross.
keywords: Pharmacies (apotheek/pharmacie) are widely available and marked with a green cross.

title: English-Speaking Clinics
snippet: Several clinics in Brussels cater specifically to the international and expat community.
keywords: Several clinics in Brussels cater specifically to the international and expat community.

title: Universities
snippet: Belgium hosts several highly-ranked universities, including KU Leuven (founded 1425), Ghent University, ULB and VUB (Brussels), and UCLouvain.
keywords: Belgium hosts several highly-ranked universities, including KU Leuven (founded 1425), Ghent University, ULB and VUB (Brussels), and UCLouvain.

title: Student Visa (Type D)
snippet: Indian students admitted to a Belgian institution need a long-stay Type D student visa, applied for through VFS Global after receiving an unconditional admission letter.
keywords: Indian students admitted to a Belgian institution need a long-stay Type D student visa, applied for through VFS Global after receiving an unconditional admission letter.

title: Student Discounts
snippet: Student cards (often the university ID) unlock discounts on transport, museums and more.
keywords: Student cards (often the university ID) unlock discounts on transport, museums and more.

title: Libraries
snippet: University and public libraries are generally open to residents and students, often with extended hours during exam periods.
keywords: University and public libraries are generally open to residents and students, often with extended hours during exam periods.

title: Work Permit / Single Permit
snippet: Since January 2019, work and residence authorisation for non-EU nationals staying over 90 days is combined into a single unified 'Single Permit' (Type B for standard employees), applied for by the employer through the relevant region's one-stop counter.
keywords: Since January 2019, work and residence authorisation for non-EU nationals staying over 90 days is combined into a single unified 'Single Permit' (Type B for standard employees), applied for by the employer through the relevant region's one-stop counter.

title: Taxes
snippet: Belgium has a progressive personal income tax system administered by the FPS Finance (Federal Public Service Finance / SPF Finances); residents file an annual tax return.
keywords: Belgium has a progressive personal income tax system administered by the FPS Finance (Federal Public Service Finance / SPF Finances); residents file an annual tax return.

title: Banking
snippet: Opening a Belgian bank account is usually needed for salary payment and daily life.
keywords: Opening a Belgian bank account is usually needed for salary payment and daily life.

title: Salary
snippet: Salary levels vary significantly by sector, role and region — Brussels and Antwerp generally offer higher wages than smaller cities, and regional salary thresholds also affect Single Permit eligibility.
keywords: Salary levels vary significantly by sector, role and region — Brussels and Antwerp generally offer higher wages than smaller cities, and regional salary thresholds also affect Single Permit eligibility.

title: Social Security
snippet: Employees and employers contribute to Belgium's social security system, covering healthcare, pensions and unemployment benefits.
keywords: Employees and employers contribute to Belgium's social security system, covering healthcare, pensions and unemployment benefits.

title: Emergency Numbers
snippet: 112 for general emergencies, 101 for police, 100 for ambulance/fire. See the Emergency Contacts section for the full list.
keywords: 112 for general emergencies, 101 for police, 100 for ambulance/fire. See the Emergency Contacts section for the full list.

title: Lost Passport
snippet: Report a lost or stolen passport to the local police first to get an official report, then contact the Embassy of India in Brussels for an emergency certificate or replacement.
keywords: Report a lost or stolen passport to the local police first to get an official report, then contact the Embassy of India in Brussels for an emergency certificate or replacement.

title: Lost Wallet
snippet: Act quickly to block bank cards and report the loss.
keywords: Act quickly to block bank cards and report the loss.

title: Scam Awareness
snippet: Belgium is generally safe, but be alert to common tourist-area scams.
keywords: Belgium is generally safe, but be alert to common tourist-area scams.

title: Passport
snippet: Must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your planned departure from the Schengen area, issued within the last 10 years, with at least 2 blank pages.
keywords: Must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your planned departure from the Schengen area, issued within the last 10 years, with at least 2 blank pages.

title: OCI (Overseas Citizen of India)
snippet: Persons of Indian origin who have taken foreign citizenship can apply for an OCI card, which functions as a lifelong multi-entry visa to India.
keywords: Persons of Indian origin who have taken foreign citizenship can apply for an OCI card, which functions as a lifelong multi-entry visa to India.

title: PAN / Address Proof
snippet: Useful to retain for financial and tax purposes back in India, especially if you'll continue to have Indian income or investments.
keywords: Useful to retain for financial and tax purposes back in India, especially if you'll continue to have Indian income or investments.

title: Schengen Travel Insurance
snippet: Mandatory for the visa application — must cover at least €30,000 for medical emergencies and repatriation, valid across the whole Schengen area for your full trip duration.
keywords: Mandatory for the visa application — must cover at least €30,000 for medical emergencies and repatriation, valid across the whole Schengen area for your full trip duration.

title: Long-Stay / Resident Insurance
snippet: Once resident, you'll typically move to mandatory Belgian health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds, e.g. via providers such as Mutualités Chrétiennes/CM, Solidaris or Partenamut) rather than travel insurance.
keywords: Once resident, you'll typically move to mandatory Belgian health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds, e.g. via providers such as Mutualités Chrétiennes/CM, Solidaris or Partenamut) rather than travel insurance.

title: Currency Exchange
snippet: Belgium uses the Euro (EUR). Exchange bureaus are available at the airport and city centres, though rates are often better via ATM withdrawal.
keywords: Belgium uses the Euro (EUR). Exchange bureaus are available at the airport and city centres, though rates are often better via ATM withdrawal.

title: ATMs & Cards
snippet: Contactless debit/credit cards (Visa, Mastercard) are widely accepted across Belgium, including on public transport.
keywords: Contactless debit/credit cards (Visa, Mastercard) are widely accepted across Belgium, including on public transport.

title: International Cards
snippet: Forex/travel cards can help avoid repeated foreign transaction fees.
keywords: Forex/travel cards can help avoid repeated foreign transaction fees.

title: Banking
snippet: See the Working in Belgium section for guidance on opening a Belgian bank account as a resident.
keywords: See the Working in Belgium section for guidance on opening a Belgian bank account as a resident.

title: Clothing
snippet: Layer-friendly clothing for a climate that changes quickly through the day.
keywords: Layer-friendly clothing for a climate that changes quickly through the day.

title: Electronics
snippet: Belgium uses Type E plugs (230V) — different from India's Type C/D.
keywords: Belgium uses Type E plugs (230V) — different from India's Type C/D.

title: Documents
snippet: Keep essential documents both physically and digitally.
keywords: Keep essential documents both physically and digitally.

title: Municipality Registration
snippet: Non-EU nationals staying longer than 90 days must register their address at the local commune/gemeente within 8 working days of arrival, per Belgium's Federal Public Service (FPS) Home Affairs rules.
keywords: Non-EU nationals staying longer than 90 days must register their address at the local commune/gemeente within 8 working days of arrival, per Belgium's Federal Public Service (FPS) Home Affairs rules.

title: Residence Permit
snippet: The permit type (e.g. Single Permit for work, student permit, family reunification) determines validity and renewal conditions.
keywords: The permit type (e.g. Single Permit for work, student permit, family reunification) determines validity and renewal conditions.

title: Brussels
snippet: Belgium's capital and the de facto capital of the EU — home to the Grand Place, EU institutions, and the largest Indian community in Belgium.
keywords: Belgium's capital and the de facto capital of the EU — home to the Grand Place, EU institutions, and the largest Indian community in Belgium.

title: Antwerp
snippet: Belgium's largest port city, a global diamond trading hub with a significant Indian (especially Gujarati/Jain) business community.
keywords: Belgium's largest port city, a global diamond trading hub with a significant Indian (especially Gujarati/Jain) business community.

title: Ghent
snippet: A medieval university city with canals, a lively student population and striking Gothic architecture.
keywords: A medieval university city with canals, a lively student population and striking Gothic architecture.

title: Bruges
snippet: A UNESCO World Heritage old town famed for its canals, chocolate shops and medieval charm.
keywords: A UNESCO World Heritage old town famed for its canals, chocolate shops and medieval charm.

title: Leuven
snippet: Home to KU Leuven, one of Europe's oldest universities, and a large international student population.
keywords: Home to KU Leuven, one of Europe's oldest universities, and a large international student population.

title: Liège
snippet: A historic industrial city on the Meuse river with a French-speaking university community.
keywords: A historic industrial city on the Meuse river with a French-speaking university community.

title: Namur
snippet: Capital of Wallonia, known for its citadel overlooking the Meuse and Sambre rivers.
keywords: Capital of Wallonia, known for its citadel overlooking the Meuse and Sambre rivers.

title: Mechelen
snippet: A charming, walkable city midway between Brussels and Antwerp with a well-preserved historic centre.
keywords: A charming, walkable city midway between Brussels and Antwerp with a well-preserved historic centre.

title: Grand Place / Grote Markt
snippet: UNESCO World Heritage central square surrounded by guildhalls and the Town Hall.
keywords: UNESCO World Heritage central square surrounded by guildhalls and the Town Hall.

title: Atomium
snippet: Iconic 1958 World's Fair structure with an observation deck and exhibitions.
keywords: Iconic 1958 World's Fair structure with an observation deck and exhibitions.

title: Manneken Pis
snippet: Brussels' famous small bronze fountain statue.
keywords: Brussels' famous small bronze fountain statue.

title: Royal Palace of Brussels
snippet: Official palace of the King of the Belgians, open to visitors in summer.
keywords: Official palace of the King of the Belgians, open to visitors in summer.

title: Parc du Cinquantenaire
snippet: Grand park with triumphal arch and museums, popular for walks and picnics.
keywords: Grand park with triumphal arch and museums, popular for walks and picnics.

title: Indian restaurants around Ixelles & Saint-Josse
snippet: Highest concentration of Indian restaurants and grocery stores in Belgium.
keywords: Highest concentration of Indian restaurants and grocery stores in Belgium.

title: City Centre / Grand Place area
snippet: Walkable to major sights, can be noisy on weekends.
keywords: Walkable to major sights, can be noisy on weekends.

title: EU Quarter / Schuman
snippet: Business-friendly, quieter evenings, close to Embassy of India.
keywords: Business-friendly, quieter evenings, close to Embassy of India.

title: Antwerp-Centraal Station
snippet: A landmark building nicknamed the 'Railway Cathedral'.
keywords: A landmark building nicknamed the 'Railway Cathedral'.

title: Grote Markt
snippet: Historic market square with guild houses and the Brabo Fountain.
keywords: Historic market square with guild houses and the Brabo Fountain.

title: Cathedral of Our Lady
snippet: Belgium's largest Gothic cathedral, home to Rubens masterpieces.
keywords: Belgium's largest Gothic cathedral, home to Rubens masterpieces.

title: Diamond District
snippet: The world-famous diamond trading quarter, home to many Indian-run diamond businesses.
keywords: The world-famous diamond trading quarter, home to many Indian-run diamond businesses.

title: Indian & Jain restaurants near the Diamond District
snippet: Several dedicated vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants serve the diamond trading community.
keywords: Indian & Jain restaurants near the Diamond District
Several dedicated vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants serve the diamond trading community.

title: Near Antwerp-Centraal / Diamond District
snippet: Convenient for business travellers and the diamond trade.
keywords: Convenient for business travellers and the diamond trade.

title: Gravensteen (Castle of the Counts)
snippet: Built around 1180 for the Counts of Flanders, this is the only fully preserved moated castle in Flanders. An audio guide covers its medieval history.
keywords: Built around 1180 for the Counts of Flanders, this is the only fully preserved moated castle in Flanders. An audio guide covers its medieval history.

title: Graslei & Korenlei
snippet: Historic twin quays along the Leie river lined with 12th-century guild houses — the postcard view of Ghent, especially at night when the buildings are lit.
keywords: Historic twin quays along the Leie river lined with 12th-century guild houses — the postcard view of Ghent, especially at night when the buildings are lit.

title: St. Bavo's Cathedral
snippet: Gothic cathedral housing the Ghent Altarpiece ('The Adoration of the Mystic Lamb') by Jan van Eyck, one of the most important works in Western art history.
keywords: Gothic cathedral housing the Ghent Altarpiece ('The Adoration of the Mystic Lamb') by Jan van Eyck, one of the most important works in Western art history.

title: Belfry of Ghent
snippet: At roughly 91 metres, the tallest belfry in Belgium, built in the 14th century as a symbol of civic independence; part of the UNESCO-listed Belfries of Belgium and France.
keywords: At roughly 91 metres, the tallest belfry in Belgium, built in the 14th century as a symbol of civic independence; part of the UNESCO-listed Belfries of Belgium and France.

title: Indian and international restaurants near the university district
snippet: Ghent's large student population supports a diverse, affordable food scene around Overpoort and the city centre; dedicated Indian restaurant options are more limited than in Brussels or Antwerp.
keywords: Indian and international restaurants near the university district
Ghent's large student population supports a diverse, affordable food scene around Overpoort and the city centre; dedicated Indian restaurant options are more limited than in Brussels or Antwerp.

title: Historic Centre (Patershol / Korenmarkt)
snippet: Walkable to Gravensteen, Graslei and the main squares; generally cheaper than equivalent Bruges hotels.
keywords: Walkable to Gravensteen, Graslei and the main squares; generally cheaper than equivalent Bruges hotels.

title: Markt Square & Belfry
snippet: Central square dominated by the 13th-century Belfry tower.
keywords: Central square dominated by the 13th-century Belfry tower.

title: Bruges Canals
snippet: Boat tours offering views of historic bridges and facades.
keywords: Boat tours offering views of historic bridges and facades.

title: Basilica of the Holy Blood
snippet: Romanesque and Gothic basilica housing a relic venerated since the Crusades.
keywords: Romanesque and Gothic basilica housing a relic venerated since the Crusades.

title: Vegetarian-friendly cafés
snippet: Fewer Indian restaurants than Brussels/Antwerp — most visitors day-trip in from larger cities.
keywords: Fewer Indian restaurants than Brussels/Antwerp — most visitors day-trip in from larger cities.

title: Historic Centre
snippet: Charming but can be crowded with day-trippers; book well ahead in summer.
keywords: Charming but can be crowded with day-trippers; book well ahead in summer.

title: KU Leuven University Library
snippet: Leuven's landmark building, with a famous Art Deco reading hall (weekends only) and a bell tower reachable via ~289 steps for panoramic city views.
keywords: Leuven's landmark building, with a famous Art Deco reading hall (weekends only) and a bell tower reachable via ~289 steps for panoramic city views.

title: Oude Markt
snippet: A historic square packed with bars and cafés, popularly known as 'Europe's longest bar'.
keywords: A historic square packed with bars and cafés, popularly known as 'Europe's longest bar'.

title: Kruidtuin (Botanical Garden)
snippet: Belgium's oldest botanical garden, established in 1783 by KU Leuven for medical students; free entry, around 800 plant species.
keywords: Belgium's oldest botanical garden, established in 1783 by KU Leuven for medical students; free entry, around 800 plant species.

title: Restaurants and cafés around the student district
snippet: A large international and Indian student population supports a growing range of South Asian grocery and food options near the university area.
keywords: Restaurants and cafés around the student district
A large international and Indian student population supports a growing range of South Asian grocery and food options near the university area.

title: City Centre (near Oude Markt / Grote Markt)
snippet: Central and walkable to the university library and main square; can be lively/noisy near Oude Markt on weekends.
keywords: City Centre (near Oude Markt / Grote Markt)
Central and walkable to the university library and main square; can be lively/noisy near Oude Markt on weekends.

title: Liège-Guillemins Station
snippet: A striking modern railway station designed by Santiago Calatrava, notable for its steel-and-glass canopy.
keywords: A striking modern railway station designed by Santiago Calatrava, notable for its steel-and-glass canopy.

title: Montagne de Bueren
snippet: A 374-step staircase built 1875-1880, honouring the defenders of Liège against Charles the Bold; rewards climbers with panoramic city views.
keywords: A 374-step staircase built 1875-1880, honouring the defenders of Liège against Charles the Bold; rewards climbers with panoramic city views.

title: Grand Curtius Museum
snippet: A museum of art, archaeology and decorative arts housed in a historic mansion overlooking the Meuse.
keywords: A museum of art, archaeology and decorative arts housed in a historic mansion overlooking the Meuse.

title: La Batte Market
snippet: One of Belgium's oldest and largest markets, held every Sunday along the Meuse riverbank.
keywords: One of Belgium's oldest and largest markets, held every Sunday along the Meuse riverbank.

title: Restaurants near the city centre
snippet: Fewer dedicated Indian restaurants than Brussels or Antwerp; the university population supports a broader international food scene.
keywords: Fewer dedicated Indian restaurants than Brussels or Antwerp; the university population supports a broader international food scene.

title: City Centre (near Place du Marché)
snippet: Central location near the historic market square and riverside cafés.
keywords: Central location near the historic market square and riverside cafés.

title: Citadel of Namur
snippet: A vast hilltop fortress with ~7km of underground tunnels, guided tours, a tourist train, and a cable car connecting it to the city centre.
keywords: A vast hilltop fortress with ~7km of underground tunnels, guided tours, a tourist train, and a cable car connecting it to the city centre.

title: Saint-Loup Church
snippet: A Baroque church in the historic centre, considered one of the finest examples of Baroque art in the former southern Low Countries.
keywords: A Baroque church in the historic centre, considered one of the finest examples of Baroque art in the former southern Low Countries.

title: Restaurants in the historic centre
snippet: Limited dedicated Indian dining options; best explored as a day trip combined with a meal in Brussels or Liège.
keywords: Limited dedicated Indian dining options; best explored as a day trip combined with a meal in Brussels or Liège.

title: City Centre
snippet: Walkable to the old town and within reach of the Citadel via cable car or on foot.
keywords: Walkable to the old town and within reach of the Citadel via cable car or on foot.

title: St. Rumbold's Tower
snippet: A 97-metre UNESCO-listed belfry; climb 538 steps (guided) for a skywalk and panoramic views. Uniquely has two 49-bell carillons.
keywords: A 97-metre UNESCO-listed belfry; climb 538 steps (guided) for a skywalk and panoramic views. Uniquely has two 49-bell carillons.

title: St. Rumbold's Cathedral
snippet: Free to enter, the cathedral beneath the tower is worth exploring for its art and architecture.
keywords: Free to enter, the cathedral beneath the tower is worth exploring for its art and architecture.

title: Béguinages
snippet: Mechelen is unique in Belgium for having two UNESCO World Heritage béguinages.
keywords: Mechelen is unique in Belgium for having two UNESCO World Heritage béguinages.

title: Grote Markt
snippet: Mechelen's central market square, a natural starting point for exploring the historic centre.
keywords: Mechelen's central market square, a natural starting point for exploring the historic centre.

title: Restaurants in the historic centre
snippet: Limited dedicated Indian dining; easiest as a day trip combined with a meal in Brussels or Antwerp.
keywords: Limited dedicated Indian dining; easiest as a day trip combined with a meal in Brussels or Antwerp.

title: City Centre (near Grote Markt)
snippet: A quieter, lower-cost alternative to staying in Brussels or Antwerp, with fast rail access to both.
keywords: A quieter, lower-cost alternative to staying in Brussels or Antwerp, with fast rail access to both.
